The **2025 NRL season** was the 118th season of professional [rugby league](/wiki/Rugby_league "Rugby league") in Australia and the 28th season run by the [National Rugby League](/wiki/National_Rugby_League "National Rugby League") (NRL). The regular season was followed by a finals series contested by the top eight teams on the competition ladder. The [Canberra Raiders](/wiki/Canberra_Raiders "Canberra Raiders") were the minor premiers, while the [Brisbane Broncos](/wiki/Brisbane_Broncos "Brisbane Broncos") won their seventh premiership.

The season recorded the highest aggregate and average regular season crowds in first-grade rugby league history, surpassing the record set the previous year.

## Teams

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2025_NRL_season&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Teams")]

The lineup of teams in the league remained unchanged for the 3rd consecutive year, with seventeen participating in the regular season: ten from [New South Wales](/wiki/New_South_Wales "New South Wales"), four from [Queensland](/wiki/Queensland "Queensland") and one from each of [Victoria](/wiki/Victoria_(state) "Victoria (state)"), the [Australian Capital Territory](/wiki/Australian_Capital_Territory "Australian Capital Territory") and [New Zealand](/wiki/New_Zealand "New Zealand").

## Player statistics and records

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2025_NRL_season&action=edit&section=13 "Edit section: Player statistics and records")]

* In Round 7, [Nathan Cleary](/wiki/Nathan_Cleary "Nathan Cleary") surpassed [Ryan Girdler](/wiki/Ryan_Girdler "Ryan Girdler") as the highest scoring [Penrith Panthers](/wiki/Penrith_Panthers "Penrith Panthers") player of all time.
* In Round 13, [Alex Johnston](/wiki/Alex_Johnston_(rugby_league) "Alex Johnston (rugby league)") became the first player in the NRL era (1998–present), and second of all time, to score 200 career tries.
* In Round 14, [Kayal Iro](/wiki/Kayal_Iro "Kayal Iro") became the first player in the NRL era to score a try in their first nine games of a season.
* In Round 14, [Josh Papali'i](/wiki/Josh_Papali%27i "Josh Papali'i") surpassed [Jason Croker](/wiki/Jason_Croker "Jason Croker") as the most capped [Canberra Raiders](/wiki/Canberra_Raiders "Canberra Raiders") player of all time.
* In Round 27, [James Fisher-Harris](/wiki/James_Fisher-Harris "James Fisher-Harris") scored a try in the first 9 seconds of a match, the fastest in NRL history.
* No player was [sent off](/wiki/List_of_players_sent_off_in_National_Rugby_League_matches "List of players sent off in National Rugby League matches") throughout the entire season for the first time since 2017.
